title: Phase 0 dispatched as a single lead, not a parallel fan-out
rationale: Ovyro's repo was empty at kickoff (only projectspec.html + tooling). The spec's 7 phases look parallelizable but are not: P1 needs P0, P2 needs P1 (leads FK listings), P4 needs P2 (leads carry meta_event_id), P5 needs P4. More importantly, any parallel builder on an empty repo scaffolds its own package.json/tsconfig/next.config, guaranteeing merge conflicts on the exact files that define the build. Dispatched ONE lead for OVYRO-34b8 with explicit instruction to land the scaffold as a single commit BEFORE fanning out builders onto disjoint areas (drizzle schema / auth / worker+queue / infra+seed). Fan-out becomes safe only once the shared scaffold exists.

description: Builder sessions spawned in nested worktrees inherit the CANONICAL repo root .claude/settings.local.json coordinator hooks: those PreToolUse hooks deny Write/Edit unconditionally and deny most file-creating Bash whenever OVERSTORY_AGENT_NAME is non-empty, so builder-scaffold (OVYRO-4a5c) could not create any file in its own worktree. Denial text: 'coordinator agents cannot modify files'. The worktree-local builder hooks (path-boundary only) are correct; the leak is settings scope, not the overlay.
resolution: Scope coordinator hooks to the coordinator: each hook guard in the canonical settings.local.json should exit 0 unless OVERSTORY_AGENT_NAME equals 'coordinator' (currently it exits 0 only when the var is empty). Alternatively spawn builders so their Claude session loads only the worktree .claude settings. Reported via urgent mail msg-n8f45jl73gi9 to coordinator; lead-foundation was already terminal.

title: Halted Ovyro Phase 0 dispatch pending hook-scope fix; do not re-dispatch builders until fixed
rationale: Verified at the settings file itself, not just from the builder's report: .claude/settings.local.json at the canonical repo root carries coordinator-profile PreToolUse deny hooks (Write/Edit/NotebookEdit/mutating-Bash) guarded only by '[ -z $OVERSTORY_AGENT_NAME ] && exit 0', which skips enforcement only for empty (human) sessions. Overstory worktrees are nested inside the repo, so every spawned agent inherits this root settings file and no builder can create a file. Two agents burned producing zero code. Re-dispatching builders before the guard is rescoped to '[ $OVERSTORY_AGENT_NAME != coordinator ] && exit 0' will fail identically every time — the correct coordinator response is to halt dispatch and escalate to the human, not to retry or to seek hook bypasses. Path-boundary hooks (worktree-scoped) are correct and must be left intact. Planning state is preserved and needs no rework: OVYRO-34b8 epic, OVYRO-4a5c scaffold spec, phases 1-6 dependency-chained, group ovyro-v1-build.

content: The canonical .claude/settings.local.json PreToolUse hooks were rescoped (as of 2026-07-21 23:32) from unconditional coordinator denies to path-boundary-only checks, unblocking builder agents in worktrees - mx-e15887 no longer reproduces. One residual sharp edge: the Bash path-boundary hook false-positives on 'git commit -F -' with a heredoc, rejecting it as a boundary violation. Use repeated -m flags for multi-paragraph commit messages instead.
